originalTitle: JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon made bearish call on treasury bond market. Many investors already acted on it
summary: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon warned this week long-term treasuries are not a good buy, even if stocks fall. Many investors already acted on that message this year.
content:
Investors concerned about the stock market and looking for ways to prepare their portfolio for a potential equities downturn typically turn to the U.S. treasuries market as a "flight to safety" allocation. But if you followed the dual stock and bond market warning issued this week by JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon , you better keep that treasuries exposure short.

In fact, that's exactly what many investors already have been doing over the past year and continued to do in June and July. While investors continue to add record sums to equity ETFs — the U.S. ETF market reached over $1 trillion in assets at the mid-year mark and equity ETFs took in almost half of that total — many investors made their own call to stay at the short-end of the treasury market.

ETF flows data over the past one year shows that investors have been piling into short-term treasuries funds, with the iShares 0-3 Month Treasury Bond ETF (SGOV) taking in more money from investors this year than any other bond ETF. The ETF has added $47.5 billion in net inflows from investors, according to ETFAction.com. It has become the third-biggest bond ETF overall, at close to $100 billion in assets, surpassed only by the Vanguard Total Bond Market ETF (BND) and iShares Core US Aggregate Bond ETF (AGG).

In an interview with CNBC Contributor Wilfred Frost on Monday, Dimon said that in addition to stocks trading at values which he wouldn't touch, he also wouldn't purchase long-dated treasuries . "The 10-year bond should probably be at 4% to 4.5%," he said.

Even if inflation begins to fall back closer to the Federal Reserve's 2% target, the CEO of the nation's biggest bank says he does not see much upside for prices in the long-dated government bonds.

The 10-year treasury is currently yielding 4.6%, and the yield has moved higher for most of the year as the market flipped from a view that the Fed would eventually lower rates to a view that a hike may be more likely . As long as the risk of a rate hike remains and the inflation outlook remains unclear, 10-year treasury prices, which move inverse to yields, should remain under pressure. Broader concerns about public spending and deficit levels also contribute to concerns about yields.

Over the past year, the Vanguard Total Bond Market and the iShares 0-3 Month Treasury Bond ETF are the only fixed-income ETFs to be among the top 10 ETFs for flows, according to ETFAction data. The iShares short-term treasuries fund is No. 5 overall, with its nearly $50 billion in net inflows. That is surpassed only by the biggest ETFs of all — Vanguard, iShares and State Street core S&P 500 funds, and the Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF.

The short-term treasuries momentum has continued throughout the year, with SGOV ranking No. 5 among all ETFs in flows for the month of June.

The idea that short-term treasuries are the place to be to smooth out market volatility is not new, and was most famously backed by Warren Buffett, who wrote in his 2013 annual letter to Berkshire Hathaway investors that his estate plan for his wife was 90% S&P 500 and 10% short-term treasuries, and that was likely good enough for most long-term investors.

Here's a look at other stories offering insight on ETFs for investors.
chineseTitle: 摩根大通的杰米·戴蒙(Jamie Dimon)对国债市场做出了看跌的预测。许多投资者已经采取行动
chineseSummary: 摩根大通首席执行官杰米·戴蒙本周警告说，即使股市下跌，长期国债也不是一个好的购买选择。今年许多投资者已经根据这一信息采取了行动。
chineseContent:
关注股市并寻求让投资组合应对潜在股市低迷的方法的投资者通常会转向美国国债市场，将其作为“逃向安全”的配置。但如果你遵循摩根大通首席执行官杰米戴蒙本周发布的股票和债券市场双重警告，你最好保持短期国债敞口。

事实上，这正是许多投资者在过去一年中一直在做的事情，并在六月和七月继续做。虽然投资者继续向股票 ETF 添加创纪录的资金——美国 ETF 市场的资产在年中达到了超过 1 万亿美元，而股票 ETF 几乎占据了总资产的一半——但许多投资者自己也呼吁留在国债市场的空头市场。

过去一年的 ETF 流量数据显示，投资者纷纷涌入短期国债基金，iShares 0-3 个月国债 ETF (SGOV) 今年从投资者那里吸收的资金比任何其他债券 ETF 都多。根据 ETFAction.com 的数据，该 ETF 的投资者净流入增加了 475 亿美元。它已成为第三大债券 ETF，资产接近 1000 亿美元，仅次于 Vanguard Total Bond Market ETF (BND) 和 iShares Core US Aggregate Bond ETF (AGG)。

戴蒙周一在接受 CNBC 撰稿人威尔弗雷德·弗罗斯特 (Wilfred Frost) 采访时表示，除了他不会碰触的股票交易价格外，他也不会购买长期国债。 “10年期债券的利率可能应为4%至4.5%，”他说。

美国最大银行的首席执行官表示，即使通胀开始回落至接近美联储 2% 的目标，他也不认为长期政府债券的价格有太大上涨空间。

10年期国债目前的收益率为4.6%，随着市场从美联储最终会降息的观点转变为更有可能加息的观点，该收益率在今年的大部分时间里都在走高。只要加息风险依然存在且通胀前景仍不明朗，与收益率走势相反的10年期国债价格就应该继续承受压力。对公共支出和赤字水平的更广泛担忧也加剧了对收益率的担忧。

根据 ETFAction 数据，过去一年，Vanguard Total Bond Market 和 iShares 0-3 个月国债 ETF 是唯一进入流量前 10 名 ETF 的固定收益 ETF。 iShares短期国债基金总体排名第五，净流入近500亿美元。只有规模最大的 ETF——Vanguard、iShares 和 State Street 核心标准普尔 500 基金，以及 Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF 才能超越这一数字。

短期国债势头全年持续，SGOV 在 6 月份所有 ETF 流量中排名第五。

短期国债是平息市场波动的场所这一观点并不新鲜，最著名的是沃伦·巴菲特(Warren巴菲特)的支持，他在2013年致伯克希尔·哈撒韦公司投资者的年度信中写道，他为妻子的遗产计划是90%的标准普尔500指数和10%的短期国债，这对于大多数长期投资者来说可能已经足够了。

以下是为投资者提供有关 ETF 见解的其他故事。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
